expectations.evidence: The reply lists 7 numbered questions across three categories (Q1-Q7).
expectations.text: The answer is a set of at least four distinct questions.

expectations.evidence: Q3 asks about frequency options, Q4 asks about 'overnight'/'next morning' timezone handling, Q5 asks about failure/retry handling, and Q6 asks about PDF vs plain data table — all directly tied to spec specifics.
expectations.text: At least three questions are grounded in specifics of this spec (e.g. overnight timing or time zones, CSV vs PDF, frequency options, failure/retry, large exports, who can access the data, retention).

expectations.evidence: Q2 bundles two distinct asks into one numbered item: 'Is this tied to one specific dashboard, or can a user choose which dashboard to export — and can they have more than one schedule active at once?' combines dashboard selection with concurrent-schedule limits.
expectations.text: No single question crams two unrelated asks together — each question carries one idea.

expectations.evidence: Questions reference concrete spec details like 'PDF format... visually replicate the dashboard's charts' and '"Overnight" and "next morning"' timing, not generic boilerplate.
expectations.text: The questions are specific to this feature rather than a generic export checklist.
